headline: Beans overdue 2 days; run zones Tuesday morning.
weather_notes: Run both zones Tuesday morning — 2 days dry at 95°F will stress 16-day tomato transplants and peak-growth beans.
Do all outdoor work before 9 AM through Thursday — zero-rain forecast with 94–97°F daily highs closes every afternoon window.
Check potato bag moisture daily — sustained 95–97°F accelerates bag drying 2–3× faster than ground beds.

surfaced_tasks.title: Feed c2–c4 bush beans this morning
surfaced_tasks.reason: 2 days overdue — 39-day beans have no fertilizer logged and need nitrogen for pod set; apply fish emulsion early before today's 97°F heat peaks.

suggested_tasks.title: Check w4 onion tops for harvest signal
suggested_tasks.description: At 72 days with no logged check, Yellow Sweet Spanish may be approaching maturity — look for fallen tops signaling it's time to pull and cure.
